Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Mar-20-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. CAMRAN PAKBAZ, Esq. of Quill & Arrow LLP (tel:310-933-4271) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Hyundai Motor America's motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is denied.

Plaintiff Francis William Byrne purchased a vehicle from a retailer in May 2019, executing a contract which included an agreement to arbitrate. Byrne filed a complaint in October 2023 alleging several statutory violations. Hyundai Motor America (HMA) now files this motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings, alleging that it has standing to bring the motion on the grounds of equitable estoppel.

The court rejects HMA equitable estoppel argument because Byrne's claims are not '"intimately founded in and intertwined' with the underlying contract obligations." (Yeh v. Superior Court of Contra Costa County (2023) 95 Cal.App.5th 264, 272; Ochoa v. Ford Motor Company (2023) 89 Cal.App.5th 1324, 1333; Kielar v. Superior Court (2023) 94 Cal.App.5th 614, 620; Montemayor v. Ford Motor Co. (2023) 92 Cal.App.5th 958, 971 (all disagreeing with Felisilda v. FCA US LLC (2020) 53 Cal.App.5th 486).) Byrne's complaint does not reference or rely on the financing agreement. Byrne is trying to enforce HMA's statutory obligations under the Song-Beverly Act to adequately repair, replace, or repurchase the car at issue. The court declines to stay the matter pending the California Supreme Court's review of Ochoa.

Because HMA does not have standing to bring the motion to compel arbitration, the motion is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-26-2024 Continued To May-01-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
